reason: Wire SCHEDULER_SERVICE_API_KEY into brightbot-slack-server so the routine Schedule/Dismiss buttons work. The deployed staging slack-server ran with an empty key (never passed through CI), so every click failed with not_configured. Value copied from the existing platform-core secret; no AWS secret was mutated — this records the source-of-truth provenance and the GitHub-secret add.

source_of_truth.note: Value NOT captured — write-only discipline. The sha256 prefix lets a future audit confirm the GitHub secret still matches this source without exposing either value.

targets_written.name: SCHEDULER_SERVICE_API_KEY
targets_written.consumed_by: .github/workflows/deploy.yml -> scripts/sync-pulumi-config.sh -> pulumi config schedulerServiceApiKey -> ECS task env
targets_written.must_match: platform-core SCHEDULER_SERVICE_API_KEY (both staging and prod) — mismatch = 401/FORBIDDEN on scheduleRoutineSuggestion

rollback: gh secret delete SCHEDULER_SERVICE_API_KEY --repo brighthive/brightbot-slack-server   # reverts to pre-BH-1063 state (routine buttons return not_configured again). To rotate: re-copy from staging/platform/platform-core current version.
